spec_match.rationale: The migration page covers the Chrome 151 support removal, Chrome version matrix, enterprise guidance, and now renders hardware eligibility from a colocated data file.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified the JSON-backed hardware rows render and the scenario focus controls are present.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page aligns with its migration-guide promise: hardware compatibility, OS upgrade paths, and enterprise fleet guidance.

edge_cases.rationale: The previous static hardware-table gap is resolved by hardware-compatibility.json and a runtime fetch/render path with a failure row.

references.rationale: The see-also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5077742779498496, Google support, and Apple compatibility references.

design_system.rationale: The data-source note and generated table use existing tokens and no raw hex colors.

summary: Re-tested in Chrome Canary through chrome-devtools-mcp at /v151/chrome-will-remove-support-for-macos-12/migration-guide/: hardware-compatibility.json loaded as six table rows, the data-source note rendered, walkthrough buttons were available, and console/network output was clean.
